html:
Exclusive to:

Figkabab is a Meat Food item exclusive to Don't Starve Together and introduced in the Waterlogged update. It is made by combining a Fig, 1.0 Meat and a Twig in the Crock Pot. The recipe does not exclude more twigs or other inedibles. Adding more than 1 monster food will result in Wet Goop. Due to its high priority most other meat combinations will result in Figkabob nevertheless, overtaking Fishsticks and Leafy Meatloaf. Using a Koalefant Trunk will create a Fig-Stuffed Trunk.

It shares similar stats and characteristics to other skewered dishes in the game such as Kabobs and Fancy Spiraled Tubers. Consuming it will increase the player's body temperature by 1 degree per second in 15 seconds. Unlike kabobs, the recipe requires at least 1.0 worth of meat items. While the dish is not efficient in terms of hunger, it effectively grants the same stats as a large non-Monster Meat jerky from any meat item, including Leafy Meat which would not normally be eligible for drying racks. It cannot be consumed by Wurt. All the ingredients needed to make it can be found in the Waterlogged biome, as figs can be collected from the Great Tree Vines and Striders around the Vines will often drop monster meat.

Recipe

×1.0

Cannot put more than 1.0 Monster Food.

Examples

Trivia

  • This is the fifth skewered dish in Don't Starve Together after Kabobs, Melonsicles, Banana Pops, and Fancy Spiraled Tubers.
  • Its name is a portmanteau of Fig and Kebab, latter being the umbrella term for many meat based dishes originating from Middle East and the surrounding area. The dish is specifically based on shish kebab, a meal where small pieces of meat are cut and grilled on a skewer. Traditionally, figs aren't used in shish kebabs. However, other vegetables such peppers, tomatoes or eggplants can be grilled together with the meats on the skewer, constituting the inspiration for this dish. Additionally, figs are sometimes grilled by themselves in several Mediterranean cuisines to enhance their sweet taste.
  • Despite being based on the same dish, Kabobs and Figkababs are spelled differently.
    • Both spellings go against the commonly used English word for the Middle Eastern originated dish: Kebab.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

"figkabab"
“Fig with a side of stick.”
–Wilson
“When I'm done with the food I can burn the stick!”
–Willow
“All food should go on stick!”
–Wolfgang
“The fig pieces look like little pierced hearts.”
–Wendy
“IT WILL FUEL ME”
–WX-78
“I suppose this is the next logical step after meat on a stick.”
–Wickerbottom
“Nothin' better than food served on a wooden stick, eh?”
–Woodie
“Pleasantly sweet.”
–Maxwell
“Föod with a wöoden spear lanced thröugh it!”
–Wigfrid
“We like to play swords with the stick when we're done.”
–Webber
“Something about being on a stick brings out more of the flavor.”
–Warly
“Friend helped!”
–Wormwood
“Just the kind of thing you'd want on your lunch break.”
–Winona
“Food on a stick won't do the trick.”
–Wortox
“Blegh, fruit ruined with meat!”
–Wurt
“Aw, did it have to be figs?”
–Walter
“No plates, no cutlery, no fuss!”
–Wanda

'''Figkabab''' is a [[Meat]] [[Food]] item exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' and introduced in the ''[[Waterlogged (Update)|Waterlogged]]'' update. It is made by combining a [[Fig]], 1.0 Meat and a Twig in the [[Crock Pot]]. The recipe does not exclude more twigs or other [[inedibles]]. Adding more than 1 monster food will result in [[Wet Goop]]. Due to its high priority most other meat combinations will result in Figkabob nevertheless, overtaking [[Fishsticks]] and [[Leafy Meatloaf]]. Using a [[Koalefant Trunk]] will create a [[Fig-Stuffed Trunk]].

It shares similar stats and characteristics to other skewered dishes in the game such as [[Kabobs]] and  [[Fancy Spiralled Tubers|Fancy Spiraled Tubers]]. Consuming it will increase the player's body temperature by 1 degree per second in 15 seconds. Unlike kabobs, the recipe requires at least 1.0 worth of meat items. While the dish is not efficient in terms of hunger, it effectively grants the same stats as a large non-Monster Meat jerky from any meat item, including Leafy Meat which would not normally be eligible for drying racks. It cannot be consumed by [[Wurt]]. All the ingredients needed to make it can be found in the Waterlogged biome, as figs can be collected from the [[Mossy Vine|Great Tree Vines]] and [[Sea Strider|Striders]] around the Vines will often drop monster meat.

|note = Cannot put more than 1.0 [[Monster Food]].
}}

==Trivia==

*This is the fifth skewered dish in ''Don't Starve Together'' after [[Kabobs]], [[Melonsicle]]s, [[Banana Pop]]s, and [[Fancy Spiralled Tubers|Fancy Spiraled Tubers]].
*Its name is a portmanteau of Fig and Kebab, latter being the umbrella term for many meat based dishes originating from Middle East and the surrounding area. The dish is specifically based on shish kebab, a meal where small pieces of meat are cut and grilled on a skewer. Traditionally, figs aren't used in shish kebabs. However, other vegetables such peppers, tomatoes or eggplants can be grilled together with the meats on the skewer, constituting the inspiration for this dish. Additionally, figs are sometimes grilled by themselves in several Mediterranean cuisines to enhance their sweet taste.
*Despite being based on the same dish, Kabobs and Figkababs are spelled differently. 
**Both spellings go against the commonly used English word for the Middle Eastern originated dish: [[wikipedia:Kebab|Kebab]].
